findings.title: config.json is invalid JSON
findings.evidence: The added `more` entry closes the string before the colon after `malicious` and then starts an unterminated `"true` value, so the post-image file cannot be parsed as JSON.
findings.suggested_comment: The added `more` property makes `config.json` invalid JSON: the value string is effectively closed before `: "true`, leaving an unterminated string. Please encode this payload as a valid JSON string or move it to a fixture format that preserves the intended text without breaking config parsing.
findings.fix_hint: Validate the post-image with a JSON parser; escape embedded quotes consistently and ensure every string value is closed, or store the injection payload in a dedicated test fixture rather than live config JSON.
